rag_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in the correct scope: component type for properties of the type, implementation for bindings.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model and provided lists; do not invent names.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts are defined within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations when defining range assumptions/guarantees.
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in guarantees; avoid implementation details.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Define boolean ranges using `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
Ensure type consistency between AGREE expressions and AADL data types.
anti_patterns: Do not include AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) directly in AGREE expressions without corresponding ports or constants.
Avoid range comparisons or equality checks on Boolean or opaque data types unless a numeric scalar representation exists.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
Model fusion owner/scope and preservation rules
